text: All these reviews are true. Customer service takes anywhere from 2-3 hours for a simple concern. They steal your money and never fix account issues. I've also had some emplyee order off of my account! Run the other way!
Dylan who is a phone tech for T-Mobile was very helpful.You guys have a great employee and he had honestly deserves a raise and to be promoted.

Their customer service is the worst.  Since early this week,I've tried to cancel my Gateway.  The only thing is the reps all of a sudden could not hear or understand me when I called.  Took the gateway to the store which is a great store to deal with because the will help you with everything and anything.  They politely told me to call 611 and I stepped into the black hole of T-Mobile.  Their internet web site,on-line chat and 611 will never be used by this person AGAIN!!!  FYI,the associates working in the store also agree the customer service is garbage.  Zero stars

I strongly recommend avoiding using T-Mobile as your cellphone provider. After being a faithful paying customer for several years, they have given me what I consider is abusive treatment for the last 5 months, since I tried to cancel my plan. I have repeatedly been told what I can only believe are lies by various personnel, who repeatedly stated I owed them differing amounts of more money. The first amount, back in December, was roughly $135, which I paid immediately over the phone. He also had asked if I had any devices to return, and then corrected himself and said no returns were due. Then when I requested an email confirmation that this ‘final payment’ had zeroed out my account, he refused to send me one, saying I had to go into a store to obtain it, or wait for one in the mail. I opted to wait, and then received a bill for $500, with no details as to what it was for, in January. Again I called and was told by someone named Sean, that this was a mistake and he then placed me on hold for over thirty minutes before informing me his two managers said it was all a mistake and they would zero it out immediately and send me notice within 7-9 business days. Instead, about 2 weeks later I received another bill for $500 from a collections agency! 

This is when I went into a store and approached their manager, Sarah, who took over trying to resolve the issue. Now several months later, it still is not resolved and I have even returned not one, but two devices I had been told didn’t need to be returned - one of which is now obsolete, due to new technology! As it stands, after canceling my service the third week of December, I have been told I need to pay $135 (paid), then $500, then $86 (paid), then $405 (non-required item returned), then $111 (obsolete item returned), only to have the bills continue and the dollar amounts change, with each new inquiry, even from one of their own store managers! As it stands today, this faithful long-time customer of T-Mobile is STILL unable to get away from their abusing treatment of me - and I know if I pay them once again, it still won’t be enough! 

Avoid T-Mobile at all costs, is my advice.
After about a dozen calls, T Mobile is still trying to charge me for lines cancelled over 2 months ago. Every customer service rep says something different....turns out over 1500 similar complaints!

negative star if I could
We were excited to move to TMobile but it has been the ABSOLUTE WORST DECISION I HAVE MADE REGARDING PHONE SERVICE EVER EVER EVER. Hours and hours on the phone to set up. Wrong numbers ported to wrong temp number. RUN FAR FAR AWAY FROM TMOBILE DONT DO IT

Store 8128 W Irlo Bronson Mem Hwy Kissimmee, FL 34787.
I asked for help, Immediately the employee cut me off and did not even listen to what I have to say. He told me to go to the Apple  Store, they are not technician!  
THEY DON'T KNOW ANYTHING ABOUT PHONE! THEY CANNOT HELP ME!  BUT, IF I BOUGHT THE PHONE FROM THEIR STORE or FROM THEM, THEY WOULD HELP ME!  IS THAT SCAM?!   
You DID NOT GET THE PHONE FROM US, WE CANNOT HELP YOU!  IS THAT SCAM?! 
THEY ARE FRANCHISE! and THEY HAVE NOTHING TO DO WITH T-Mobile! 
The employee told me to get out of my store, he owns the store?! 
He was loud, almost yelling across the store, he was walking and circling around. 
I told him why are you yelling and too far, I'm right here standing right by the table near the door. 
They didn't even have customer and not busy at all. Christian came to me told me the same thing! The other guy was laughing, I told Christian, you see that employee is making fun of me and he is condescending. Christian kept cutting me off and gave me nasty attitude, he is even worse! A Manipulator!  
I was calling the T-Mobile #611, to get help while I'm inside the store. Hoping that we can resolve my phone issue, but, both employee told me to get out of their store! 
Technically, they harassed me, I was by myself and there's 2 of them and they both man. 
I told them there's a camera here,  They can see how you mistreated me!
I called the T-Mobile from my car. I told the Reps what happened in that store, I asked for Manager, she put me on hold took the info. I asked her I need to speak to manager, she can't get a manager?  
I will never ever go to this place again, I am cancelling my T-Moblie carrier and I will tell my friends and family to cancel theirs. I will tell everyone never to sign up with T-Mobile. 
HORRIBLE EMPLOYEE! HORRIBLE EXPERIENCE! HORRIBLE PEOPLE! THEY ARE VERY IMPATIENT! THEY HAVE NO COMPASSION! THEY ARE VERY DISRESPECTFUL!  
NO CUSTOMER SERVICE!  VERY RUDE!

One of the WORST customer service experiences I have had on the phone with getting a new sim card activated with them. On the phone for nearly 2 hours (30-40 minutes of wait time with background music to wait to speak to an actual person), speaking with several different customer care agents who I had to start from scratch with about the issue/process, NOT getting a callback like they said they would when a call would drop as planned, not activating the right SIM card number even though we talked through it digit by digit several times. The glaring reason why it's this bad? English second language customer service; answers/questions are missed or misinterpreted, step by steps are unclear for the customer when explained, or sometimes the information given by the customer service agent is just flat out wrong.

T mobile has been absolutely horrible experience for me. I have been with the company for four years and upgraded to the iPhone 13. I was given a price and was assured that my cost would not go up. It went up by $10-$13 dollars.  I have called every single month trying to get this straightened out and have constantly been assured that now we have fixed it and no more worries-That was just a big joke I have spoken to multiple people all assuring me that the problem is fixed but it is not. I deal on principles, ethics, integrity, and honesty all of which T-Mobile has miserably failed. As soon as my phones are paid off I will be leaving this pathetic unethical lying company. Be warned and also read most of the other reviews in this report!! Don’t fall for their Neicy nice syrupy sweet talk. It’s all a lie!
If you are past due on the bill the IVR will not allow you to even speak with a representative about anything without paying or making a payment arrangement first.

Been a customer for 7 years. WILL NOT recommend at ALL!!!!!!!! Customer service is terrible. I’ve called for the same issue for a week everyday and they tell me one thing snd it’s another thing. They constantly lie and waste their own time as well as our time. i regret ever choosing T mobile. I will never recommend T mobile to anyone ever.

T-Mobile wants you to think they have great customer service but it is all smoke and mirrors.  I talked to Danielle, a supervisor, who promised me a voucher for a new phone - but she never followed through with it.  I called to check on the voucher and Macy in CS promised to look into it and call me back in 2 days between 11am-1pm.  She never called.  Its all a game to make them look good in the short-term.  Immediately when you get off the phone with them, you receive a text to rate the service received.  They are counting on you to give them a high rating based on their false promises.  But if you wait to see if they follow through on their promises, you will find that the  text to rate them has "expired".  I am going back to Verizon.

On 5/25 I visited store at Eastgate Mall, Cin., Oh. Joe Alvarado and John Lombert assisted in changing phones for me for over 2 hrs. They were pleasant and very helpful.  I will always be a T Mobile customer!

After almost ten years, we are finally over it and are switching. $152.00 for an emergency phone call to Germany?  This is absolutely ludicrous. We didn't pay that when we had a landline thirty years ago. Good bye.

edits.text:
We have been T-mobile customers for almost ten years. We rarely had an issue, and when we did it was always quickly resolved to our satisfaction. Not so this time.   The original plan we had included free calls within North America, including Canada.  Imagine our surprise when we saw a charge on our most recent bill for almost $100 for a call to Canada!   T-mobile actually charged us $1 PER MINUTE to call Canada.  When we called, asking for an explanation, we were simply told that this plan does not "exist" anymore.  
Very disappointed with this "bait and switch" tactic--especially, after having been a customer for so long.  We are now looking for a new carrier.

text: My team of American experts are in the  Philippines. They don’t understand American culture or nuance.  They do everything they can to not support the service I pay for

T Mobile should be ashamed how they have treated me & their customers with this LG IMS stopped working issue that has caused me & millions to be without service for 2 days. Blaming LG is not the answer, you need to send an email to the affected customers explaining how you're going to fix this not trying to up sell a new phone. This is how you treat a 20 year customer, I will be moving my service somewhere else & filing a complaint with the Attorney General.

I left my previous cell phone service provider because of a billing dispute. For several months I was with T-Mobile with no complaints. I changed my service to an additional line and suddenly there are fees and taxes that did not appear before. A call to customer service was not helpful and the end result was changing services once more. T-Mobile failed, in the end they are no better than any of the other services that pillage my wallet at every opportunity.
their are the worst company i know their lie i call too many times and nothing happened everything is stuped excuses never more i try with t mobile

I needed a phone. I got good service and got what I wanted . I don't like passwords on my phone so Juan took the password off for me and gave me my senior discount and a cheaper plan. Juan was nice and explained different options I could get.

Following a relocation, I changed to T-Mobile and purchased a phone from one of its stores in February 2022.  By May, I was ready to pull my hair out after multiple trips to a local store closer by and calls to technical/customer service #611 (based overseas with observed language barriers, although extremely friendly and polite – well scripted). Issues ranged from exceptionally poor call quality and/or others unable to hear me, often repeatedly within a same call (are you there?  Can you hear me?  You went away... Etc.), to frequently dropped/disconnected calls.  It was almost redeeming when those same issues occurred during calls with T-Mobile(!). I experienced instances of inability to even place calls, receiving an error message 'network unavailable - please try your call again later'.  How is that possible with national 5G coverage?  When your cell is your only phone or connection to people in your life or work (many out of state), this is unacceptable.  There was no pattern for time of day or my physical location.  I was shipped a replacement (clearly a refurbished model given the hidden stylus was missing…) phone as one solution.  After agonizing time spent on multiple calls, downloading multiple transfer apps (CloneIt, ShareIt, Data Copy Transfer, etc.) and issues with transferring data from the original to the replacement device, the replacement phone performed no better than the originally purchased device.  Next followed almost as much effort to delete data from the replacement device and/or ensure my original phone still had all its original data.  Among the many, many and lengthy trouble shooting calls or visits (up to two hours each) to their store, I exhausted every reasonable customer due diligence to remedy those issues before realizing they could not be remedied.  Last but not least, I discovered T-Mobile embeds proprietary software to ‘lock’ devices purchased through its stores, preventing the customer from using it on another carrier network (i.e. after leaving T-Mobile) despite having fully paid for and owning the device.  This seems some type of free trade market violation, but I'm no expert.  I know I've never encountered that previously when preparing to change carriers.  After T-Mobile explaining my phone could be 'temporarily unlocked' up to five times a year for up to 30 days each, and 'permanently unlocking' the device was an exception, I suggested T-Mobile could buy back my device for what I paid or I could pursue it in small claims court.  I was then assured a permanent unlock could be accomplished.  We initiated that process with my originally purchased phone for use with a different carrier able to provide consistent, quality cell service.  And imagine that transition will require more time to complete.  I have invested an amazing amount of time and effort with T-Mobile (one of the last reps I spoke with said I had the patience of a saint given having experienced so many issues).  I wonder they grew too quickly and/or aren’t operating as robustly as touted among their heavy marketing and advertisements.  I was initially excited to change to (i.e. try) T-Mobile, yet super disappointing outcome.  In 20+ years of cell phone use with practically every other major carrier, I’ve never experienced anything like what I’ve encountered with T-Mobile.  I couldn’t give them a more enthusiastic thumbs down if I tried!

T-Mobile sold me on an upgrade and we sent in the old  phones and yet to see any credit.  Now been three weeks....  Can't contact them or leave a call back number.  I even left them a message for a call back, no call back..........  Good luck trying to reach them for anything, they simply do not answer....

Called about problems with my T-Mobile ID, waited 30 minutes for them to pick up the phone and then went through a 45 minute phone call of them telling me to try reentering the email (which has already been done) just for her to repeat my information back to me and tell me she doesnt know what is wrong and transfers me for another 30 minute wait. T-Mobile does not live up to their saying "Customer first"

Every single time I buy a new phone though t mobile I have to fight to get the credit I was promised.  I love the service but I will ever buy a phone through them again. This is my 4th phone and 1 1/2 month later, I'm fighting for my $400.00 credit. DON'T BUY their phones.

T-Mobile has horrible customer service - after you buy the phone.

I signed up for the T-Mobile Mobile Phone Plan last year. Three days later, I changed my mind. I was told by T-Mobile I was still within the window “buyer’s remorse” and I could cancel without penalty. I returned the phone and cancelled the service.

The months following, I continued to be billed by T-Mobile. I've called them at least 10 times to correct the problem, went to the South Burlington store three times spoke with the manager, and wrote to T-Mobile.  Each attempt the person I spoke with sounds surprised by the error, apologizes and assures me it will be fixed.

After several months of this, blocked them from my credit card. They continued to bill me via mail. I again made several attempts to get them to fix their error.

Now my account is with a credit agency. I am still trying to get them to correct their error. No success after EIGHT MONTHS! I have filed complaints with the State Attorney and BBB. Hopefully they can help.

Consider yourself warned.

#tmobile
#tmobilereview

I didn’t want to give even one star.  I purchased a phone for my elderly mom.  The salespeople were nice, but my experience went downhill quickly after that.  When I tried to return the phone the next day, I was told I had to come back to return phone when the exact person I purchased it from, was working. Huh??  I luckily caught them there the next day, but they failed to cancel my service, when I turned the phone in.  So another phone call to customer service to cancel.  Told all was fine, account closed, no charges.  A month later, I receive a statement.  I called customer service once again.  They couldn’t locate account to verify charges.  They told me I would have to go back into store to have them check account.  When I voiced my displeasure at this request, they politely hung up on me.  So today, I reluctantly paid the bill, just to make all remnants of such an irritating experience go away.  Would I ever consider T-Mobile again?….never…ever.

I had been a Sprint customer since 1999. I stayed with Sprint because I had limited issues compared to other providers. Ever since T-Mobile took over Sprint, I've had nothing but problems, the worst signal, service I've ever had. I miss calls because they go straight to VM, text don't come in on time, apps close or pause, Signal sucks. I've even upgraded to the S22+, No improvement..   I use my phone for personal and work. Missing calls is something I can not have happen. T-Mobile, get your crap together or I'm finally switching to someone else after 13 years of good performance with the same provider. You've screwed up a good company in Sprint

It took about a month for T-Mobile and Inseego to figure out how to send me a warranty replacement battery for my hotspot. Twice T-Mobile sent replacement hotspots WITHOUT batteries. Inseego took over 2 weeks to ship the battery. Simple warranty requests should be just that... SIMPLE and EASY to manage.
T mobile has charge for something I never order and no one from T-Mobile can’t access the account to figure this charge on the account since I’m a Verizon member now T-Mobile has the worst service

T-Mobile has contempt for its customers. I signed up for 5G Home Internet on May 1. On May 15 I began the cancellation process due to T-Mobile throttling my service. This was the beginning of a nightmarish experience with their customer service. I spent 2 hours and 36 minutes trying to cancel. I was cycled through at least 5 agents over this period of time. Had I hung up I wouldn’t have gotten it cancelled and would have had to repeat the whole ordeal. So I waited. Moreover, I read that you can’t cancel any other way. For example, you cannot bring your modem into a T-Mobile store and cancel there. They force you to do it over the phone where they know they can keep you on hold for 2 and 1/2 hours, in the hope that you give up on the cancellation process. Pretty awful tactics. No way to treat your customers.

I have been a T Mobile customer for 22 years! Tonight they email me about setting up home for 50 dollars blah blah blah. It looks like a decent deal so I said sure I'll give it a shot. I spend  10 minutes filling everything and hit submit and it gives me some weird "cannot submit". It says it's an internet issue, but I check all sites and mbps and it's at 100. So it's not the internet it's their website. I did it twice and both time same stupid error. So I'm annoyed and I call them just to make sure it went through. A guy with no clue about anything answers and he takes 40 freaking minutes to schedule it. Here's the thing the goober processed with wrong address. I've now wasted over an hour. I call and talk to next guy. Hey I just called 2 seconds ago and guy put wrong address. "Were sorry we have time delete order and start new one". 25 minutes later he finally fixes but says he will call later for rest. If you can't process online do not, I repeat do not!!! Call the support line. Nearly 2 hours wasted on a "seamless transition". Someone over there needs to understand what seamless means. I get if I had no affiliation with T-Mobile but they have been auto billing me for 20 years. So ridiculous!

TATI @ T-Mobile store 3266 John young parkway @ loop in Kissimmee Florida was amazing from beginning to end very professional and got us exactly what we were looking for. She told us of promotions which we got! Excellent customer service skills! Thanks Tati! Pamela and Jesus

I have been with T-mobile for over 10 plus years, On May 16, 2022 I notice by current was $520.11 and the normal bill would be $379.00. So I am going to question my bill.  I had been on for an hour The first person told I had not paid all of the bill and it had been carrying over for months.  Now let me say this T-Mobile will not allow to keep carrying over a past due bill.  That would be a hell to the NO.  I had spoke with 4 different people with 4 different amounts to be explaining why my cell phone bill is so high.  So now I am pissed, So CJ allows me to speak the a manager on duty. Kyle something he then gives me a credit on bill for $60.55, ok the bill is now $460.10, There after that I pay a total of $55.00 towards the $460.10 balance. My daughter goes on the T-Mobile website to schedule a payment arrangement. The system tells her, we have a past due balance. So now its May 17, 2022 I am calling them again I spoke to Camille she tells me I now need to pay additional monies of $62.81 now it's on. After all of this my balance is $341.74.  My point you would like to think Customer Service would get the bill correct and not all this back and forward mess., would not be necessary.  Check your bill and question it. No one has money to give away on a cell phone bill.  We are ready paying high gas prices.

Visiting Florida. Called in to a T-mobile shop in South Miami along highway 41. Asked if my Huawi phone or my Oppo phone will take a US sim. They put one in the Huawi and said Yes. I paid )about $60 for an over 55 plan. I traveled on to Fort Myers. Next day there was no internet nor voice calls. I called in to a T-moble shop situated along Mcgregor Blvd. They did tests, called a support line and then told me neither phone was compatible. So I asked for a refund. This was refused quoting no refunds for prepaid services. 
Totally unacceptable service.
I then visited the nearest AT&T shop who provided a sim with similar services ($50 unlimited data and calls). This worked fine for my total time in the USA (3 weeks)

Most horrible service Ive ever experienced.Ive got bounced around to  4 different people.The issue is when i got my home internet service they didnt combine my phone service with it so i was surprised when my home internet got shut off..The first person everytime he would ask me a question i wpuld.answer but got delayed responce like the connection was off..I set my service up for auto pay he said he fixed the issue but didnt.The second service rep said there was something wrong with my gateway tower and told me to take it to a store to have.it checkedout. I arrived to be told no maam you didnt pay your bill and the late fee probably.would be waived if i called them.He hands me a number on a piece.of paper.I call to find its been disconnected.I looked it up on line explained what happened and this girl has got me on hold for almost an hour.Man i wish i could screenshot the time..at the beginning of our call i could here alot of people in the background sounded like a party..dam come on t mobile..you can do better than this.

Don’t EVER EVER EVER EVER EVER GET T-MOBILE ALL THEY DO IS LIE/!!!!!!!!!!!! All of the EXPERTS there and I only found one and she is the ONLY person in the past 10 days that had done anything for me worth my while. You do better somewhere else. **through tears ** been with them 8 years, it’s time for a divorce. TODAY!!!!

I called T-mobile, on hold 2 hours, then got someone who barely spoke English, had to repeat myself, like 10 times. I was promised a promo for Ipods, a reimbursement of $65, waiting 6 months,  never received it!!!

My account suddenly was canceled.  They kept calling me since September at 10:30 at night and I kept telling them I had a change of address and I am now on the east coast time and to call me at a decent hour. They NEVER sent a letter, email or regular mail with a notice. One day after they closed this account, my phone overcharged and the battery blew up.  Coincidence?

Worst service ever! Their people lied about every time we called. Just giving you fake promise and don't even resolve the issue.
Their network sucks. Every time we called they only make excuses that they are doing maintenance on nearest tower and this how they waste your 1 hour .
I won’t recommend their service to anyone.

I purchased a plan and a phone from T mobile. The first phone was defective and I returned it and purchased a second phone from them. The service rep imported contact info using 3 different phones, put the sim card from the defective phone and I had a real mess with contacts coming and going , inability to contact family members. I spent several "HOURS" on the phone with T mobile and a Samsung specialist trying to fix the issues with the phone.  I returned the phone and then came the real "nightmare with these T mobile reps trying to cancel my service. The first attempt was obviously a rep working from her home because I could here children in the background. She kept me on the phone for a very long time trying to talk me out of canceling service. She outright refused to do so and I hung up, called again and got another rep refusing to cancel my service, suggesting I get a family member to take the phone and service. A third try was a success, however I was charged for monthly service after cancelling within 3 business days. Their contract states-within 30 days. Fortunately my credit card service is excellent and highly professional in the way they handle disputes. I think my experience with T mobile should be a warning to anyone wanting to use them, just in case you have a similar issue. They are very difficult to work with.

The only thing good i can say is that the customer service operators were beyond polite. However, the left hand never knows what the right hand is doing. On several occasions I have called 3 times in one day and have gotten 3 totally different answers and each person swears that they are right.If I wasn't stuck with this $700 phone that 2 people today said i could take back, got to the store embarrassed myself called customer service again then someone else tells me "no Ma'am you cannot" 2 1/2yrs I've been dealing with this crap. Stupid me, I should have left when i had the chance

Had a great experience when purchasing a new phone.  Genesis,  the sales professional who educated me and gave me many options to choose from was awesome! Refreshing to meet a 20somthing who is driven and overwhelmed me with information! T-mobile will loose her soon because she is unique.  Go see her if you need a phone ASAP.

Tmobile sent a phone that I didn't agree to. I had insurance through Assurance yet they elected to sent me a phone and charged me $960.  They then lowered it to $645 and I still refused. They submitted my account to collections and it has affected my credit score.

Absolute terrible customer service I've only been with them a week and have nothing but problems every time I talk to customer service after one day of having the service I have to call the complaint about slow speeds and dropping out every time tech who was on the phone said we'll have to send you out a new device gateway I said is that a guess or is there a legitimate technical reason why you think it's my gateway Then what makes it worse he's asking for my billing information I said why do you need my billing information for technical support They go required to put it on the form so I gave it to him Then when I go online he changed my billing date when I'm supposed to have my money taken out each month so I have to call customer service going to have it fixed and they told me it's going to take a couple of days very inexperienced people They are just reading from a script that's given in front of them for customer ask a question it's not on the script They get all confused and lost very unprofessional

I keep calling customer service for assistance on why I am not getting data coverage where I am supposed to be getting service. First I was not an authorized user so customer service had me get the head of account to add which I did after a day. Next time I called now I need a frigging PIN to be able to helped. I'm not asking for any info on the account and now I am an authorized user and still can't be helped. All I want to know is why I am not getting service on my Samsung S20 Plus which is 5G capable! And I'm inside the 5G coverage map which is shown in their Tmobile website!!

Horrible customer service and no way of keeping your account safe. That's what you'll get. For a week straight we were getting texts notifying us that our PIN/Password was being changed on our account. It tells you on the text to call if it wasn't you. So, we called. On the 1st day we literally couldn't understand the person. We had to be transferred and that person treated us like an idiot. He explained how to change the password (like we didn't know) and assured us that on one can get in our account but us. He didn't seem to understand that the problem was someone IS getting into our account and changing things. We went through days of this, we get the text, find out someone changed something, find out we are now locked out of our own account, call, no help...just empty assurances. On the fourth day of this we get a text saying a new person has been added to the account AND a new billing plan (they bought something). We call immediately and they tell us once again everything is fine. My husband (now pissed) keeps them on the line as he drives to a store where they say the very opposite. They pulled up the new recites showing how someone in FL bought an Iphone using our account. They tell us they have removed the man and took off the phone and that the issue will now go to their "fraud department" to be investigated. Now, we are moving out of the country in a week. We need working phones and to know they are safe. No one gives us more information than "the fraud department is working on it". We even find out there is NO WAY for T-Mobile customer service to call said fraud department nor is there a way for us to get a hold of them. You just have to wait. So we do. I get a call a few days ago from someone that sounds 100% inept. They ask me questions they should've know, took long pauses, didn't answer questions. They ask me over and over again if I know the guy in FL that bought the Iphone (I'm in MO by the way). I keep saying I don't and they reply with, "Well, I guess we'll take him off the account." I'm livid and say, "Wait, we were told days ago that he and the phone were already removed! Were they not?" They literally say, "Ummmm...I dont know...ummm...let me check...ummm." This is how they sounded the entire call. It honestly sounded fishy. They call one more time to tell me the "case is closed". I say "What is the next step, how did they get into the account, what are you doing to insure it doesn't happen again?" I get "Ummmm I don't know...". They then assure me no one can get into the account. The same way customer service had been assuring me all week (while someone was in my account). I'm not satisfied with how this fraud rep is responding and the lack of questions being answered so I ask if there is anyone else I can talk to. She says yes and then hangs up on me. Here's the super fun part! I call 611 tell multilple reps the entire story. I get hung up on 4 times. I finally get to someone and I ask for a manager. I wait 40 mins on Mothers Day while my kids are waiting to talk to this manager. She tells me there is no way for them to get ahold of thier own fraud department nor can I. Meaning, they can do what they want and I/we can't do anything about it. I also am told that it appears my case is still open even though I was told it was closed. I explain that I'm moving soon and I just need some questions answered. She tells me she will email fraud and call me today....I have yet to receive a call. 
How is this okay? How can big companies treat people like this? Please do not do business with them. Your information IS NOT SAFE!!!

Customer service is great, but the service that I’m actually paying for now is actually not being honored as it is being promoted on t.v. (5G fastest reliable network with t-mobile.)
Hate to say it but, I did better with Metro PCS & they are associated with t-mobile.

Anyways I’m only on here to gather some evidence in regards to when I decide to sue for false advertisement glad to see I’m not the only one as I’m not the type to point fingers.


Good luck everyone.!

GO TO ANOTHER STORE, AT LEAST TO ANOTHER BRANCH BUT NOT THIS one. Yesterday I went to the T Mobile store at the BROWARD MALL in Plantation THE WORST SERVICE, I had bought a memory there the previous Sunday and something had happened that in 2 days the memory was full, I went to get help, well when they saw that I didn't want buy a new phone OR smart watch or accept the internet offer but only fix the problem of the new memory because the lady who attended me left me sitting like a Garbage and went to attend to another client, It is the worst Service
Very Dishonest people

Online customer service is very hard to understand. i talked to 4 different people over the course of 2 phone calls and a chat and they gave me contradictory information. I was disconnected from the phone call at exactly the 1 hour mark, as if that’s standard procedure. Then after a 30 minute chat I was told I could only get a solution by going to the store where they said the store could take care of everything I needed. At the store they told me they couldn’t compensate me for the service I didn’t get over the phone, which is exactly what the phone people told me they could do. It’s like the right hand doesn’t know what the left is doing and what makes it worse is they acted like they didn’t care. I was going to switch my phone lines over when my contact was up but now I’m not. I will not give them any more business because of the poor customer service.

Horrible connection: little to no reception at Citrus Heights of Riverside..
No ETA on when this will be fixed..

edits.text:
Tmobile engages in dishonesty. They quote a rate (recorded) when bill comes it is a very different rate!
Also, many pockets in southern CA have no service coverage... disappointed!!
............
The review above was s one star.
Tmobile called and remdied the situation to my satisfaction.
Any company that cares to do that gets five stars!!

text:
The rep who helped me was polite and thorough but the issue I called about was not resolved, though I'm pretty sure she wasn't the only one to blame.  

When we first started service with T-mobile eleven months ago, we traded in two older version iPhones (an 8 plus & XR) for two new iPhone 13's, since T-mobile was running a promotion and we could trade straight across without having to pay the monthly fee for the new devices.  

When we got home, I realized that the sales rep in the store had given me the wrong storage (64gb instead of 128gb).  I called the store and he said they didn't have that storage version of the phone in stock so he told me to call customer service and order it through them instead.  I called customer service and ordered the device.  

Afterward, looking at the bill via the app, I could see that there was now a $30 monthly service fee for the new device, with a contract to pay off the full balance of the phone.  I called customer service again and they said this fee & contract would be removed after I received the new phone and returned the 64gb device back to the store.  I was given the same explanation by the in-store rep when I returned the 64gb device.  

Fast forward to now, after several months of preparing to sell our house, moving our family out of state (during which time I was not checking the bill details since I was overwhelmed with moving) and realizing today that we are STILL being charged for this phone $30 per month, with roughly $400 more to pay.  

After talking with a sales rep in the store, he could see the promotion was not applied appropriately for some reason to the phone and to call customer service to resolve it.  Customer service, after being placed on hold numerous times over an almost 2 HOUR phone call, said the best they can do is refund 6 months of the charges since I didn't catch this error sooner.  

So in essence, even though it was T-mobile's responsibility and error, I have to pay $800 for a phone that should have been free???  That is TERRIBLE CUSTOMER SERVICE, not to mention extremely dishonest and shady business practices!!!  Will be looking to transfer to another phone provider after our contract is up.

Was on the phone for over an hour today to find out that after buying an upgrade they do not want to facilitate 
the return of a device that I had previously paid off from them. This was after they had mailed an unrecognizable SIM card for the new device. I feel sorry for any TMobile employee, because the service they are selling and working for is not worth the lint from my dryer.

I would give zero stars if possible!  A number on my account was compromised because someone got through the 2-factor authentication and was able to turn on msisdn.  This essentially allowed multiple device connections to the one phone number.  The account shows that DIGITS is off, but the number has received thousands of texts and hundreds of phone calls as a result.  The number can’t receive sms texts from my number however.  We’ve now been dealing with this since 4/23/22 and have contacted T-Mobile support at least 10 times.  Some of their attempts at troubleshooting involved added spam blocker “features” both free and one that would have cost $4 a month per line, rebooting our network connection, and insanely enough, telling us to change the phone number.  Every contact ends with, “we’ve escalated this” and we’re so sorry this is happening”, and the most laughable, “someone will call you back, in an hour, tomorrow, in x business days”, with nothing being fixed and no return calls ever occurring.  If anything, the situation continues to get worse because their “fixes” don’t let legitimate calls/texts through.  I had to do my own troubleshooting to even determine the access change on 4/23 that started the issue and their support seems too incompetent to see the account history.  I had to point out that the device name was changed to someone named Alan Stuckey…whoever that is.  Someone was able to make a change without me getting the auth code text or providing a pin.  I’ve been a LOYAL customer for 20+ years, but seems not to matter to anyone at T-Mobile.  My next step is changing carriers and to tell EVERYONE I know not to do business with them.

Help Stop the deceptive practices & methods used by their Sales / Customer Care Team and help create a better experience for the Customer.  By taking action and raising awareness to T-Mobile Corporate, Our Voices Will Be Heard. 

PHONE NUMBER:
+1 425-383-4000
DO NOT PRESS 1 FOR CUSTOMER CARE 
PRESS 2 FOR CORPORATE.

OH MY GOD BAD! I am on the phone helping my 85 year old mother cancel her account. We have gone from owing $350 to now supposedly over $900 - WITH ZERO EXPLANATION! This is the worst customer service I have ever had. I have never been so thankful to add my mother to my Verizon account!!! Customer Service is foreign. They won't listen and this company is running a one heck of a racket. AVOID THEM LIKE THE PLAGUE! If I could give a negative rating I would!

I had t-mobile service in Sugar Land, Texas, large suburban area, had to use a cell signal booster in our home, otherwise we experienced dropped calls, then moved to Fulshear, Texas, and experienced same thing.  I spoke to customer service in both Sugar Land and Fulshear and they sent me a booster.  In Fulshear, two years in a row I was told they are working on upgrades, I asked if they were in our area and they said about 5 miles away, after another year and no improvement we switch to Sprint.  Reception is slightly better but not much. When we leave our garage we drop the signal until we get about 1 mile away.  This is a common occurrence for residence in our area.   I would not recommend t-mobile/Sprint in the Fulshear area.

They stole money out of my account and in trying to fix there mistakes now my account is saying I have a past due amount owed right now or my service will be shut off. This company is a scam and they hold you hostage with their promotion.

I am a fan of T-Mobile, they are by far one of the best mobile services I've had. However, I wish they would put up a tower in my area (Rainelle, WV) they have a site location here, and I get great network service at the store because they have a booster. I don't get good enough network coverage where I live, only about a mile from their store. My sync up drive will not update until you drive at least 30 minutes out of town. The home internet is unavailable to me because they are waiting for a tower to be put up. But other than that, I love the deals/promotions and features T-Mobile offers!

Scammed after finding added protection plan added without my knowledge and refused to allow me to purchase a new phone was told to use protection plan instead of cancelling, providing a used refurbished replacement. Then charge a “warranty fee” of $500 and 4months later stating the phone was lost, still paying on phone replaced and protection plan. As they investigate and add additional charges. To find they used a warranty that I wasn’t covered under and not to the insurance company that I pay additional charges I pay for.  I’m covered under and stating I still need to pay anyways to avoid suspension and then do a payment arrangement for the challenged billing. As double charging in 1 month billing cycle for upgrading into the magenta plan from the grand father plan.Informing me to pay as they discounted $60 from both plans as used both plans in one month and charged full price for both plans however I received a prorated amount of $60?? Mind you both monthly
Plans combined are over $400….stating that you pay for both plans due to changing during a billing period. I was informed they will reach out later today as the service is horrible and the calls continue to drop from their bad service!!!!!!

Love the customer service. They are always helpful and courteous - also, I am never on hold for a long time. Phone service is mediocre at best. Coverage still not what it should be in 2022. Worst part, as I was upgrading my phone I was offered a "free" watch. Well, after 3 months, I realized I was pay a line for a watch I actually never used. Thought I would use the watch but the Samsung watch was not for me. When I cancelled the line the watch was not "free" not only that - the same watch which Samsung has for $199 I had to pay T-Mobile $269 for just to remove the additional line from my account. My fault - free is not free but honestly, this really left a bad taste in my  mouth. Will be looking to change carriers after the new phone is paid off.

We bought an international pass to use our account outside the United States from the first day we could not make calls and after speaking with more than eight people from the company calling from Argentina we could not solve the problem, we are two elderly people for whom it was very important to have communication if an emergency of any kind had arisen, we had to use friends' phones there to be able to make local calls I don't want to imagine what would have happened in a real emergency, after a week an agent told me to reset the network and That way we were able to call but we lost internet.

 The treatment was terrible the agents in the Philippines that they have working for the company hung up on me when I was trying to solve the problem other agent named Larry left me waiting on the line when I asked him to transfer me to a supervisor even though I explained that it was urgent that we have communication because we were in a foreign country and we were elderly.

 When I arrived in the United States they gave me the credit for the $50 that we paid for the service that was terrible I can assure you that I will never use the service of T mobile outside the United States under any circumstances the experience was terrible

4.5 years with T-Mobile (four phones) and it has been a love and hate relationship. Love the price. But not the greatest receptions. Customer Service has been getting worst and worst during the past year. Horrible trade in deals for existing customers- always. Much, much better deals just going to the Apple Store or Samsung.com to trade in your old phone and buy a new one!

I had TMobile for years until we moved and no longer got service where I lived. Decided to try Sprint out but TMobile bought them out about 6 months in. As soon as the merge happened, the service got even worse; it got to the point that using wifi still wouldn't help. We decided to stick it out and pay off the phones so we could switch carriers and as soon as we paid them off they went up $45 a line (for 6 lines) on my bill because I was "no longer under contract with them since my phones were paid off" To top it off, they wouldn't unlock my devices until I paid the taxes on the phone which I had already done when I first signed on with Sprint (that way my bill would be cheaper)

I was unable to connect my phone with T-Mobile so it was cancelled, all within a few hours and never used for phone calls.  I got 2 bills from T-Mobile, called and verified acct. was cancelled.  Got a bill from their collection agency for same amount; unable to connect with that agency (call woudn't go thru).  Called T-Mobile again  with no help.

The homeinternet department promised on April 22nd the tower upgrades would be completed that didn’t happen lot one then  the 29th that didnt happen 2nd lie now msy6promissed is this going to. E lie when I call it’s always a Philippines callin station when ythey answer the phone it’s a scripted talk what don’t like when they say rest assured who talks like that people that does distract the other day a tech misunderstood what I was saying and shut my internet later the expert sai sorry tje homeinternet personal should all be fired and bring those jobs back to the unitedstates T-Mobile shut down the over seas home internet and setup a call in station here in the unitedstates otherwise a lot of people will leave T-Mobile us paying customers are your boss us paying customers with out us there’s no T-Mobile one more thing stop acting like your to big to fail your on that road right now

Please read!  I went to a Walmart store and purchased a TMobile phone/account.  Yes. TMobile person in store.  Kept phone two days and returned it.  Purchased Straight Talk and phone.  They gave me back credit so I thought for phone and service for TMobile.  Then the bills started!  I received a bill for $101.40 for those two days called, called, and called TMobile.  Told to go to TMobile store, hang ups.  Total nightmare!  I went to the TMobile store they tried for approximately 45 minutes to help me but they got the same run around hangup etc.  Today I received a bill from a debit collector.  Back on the phone telling me to go to the store.
DO NOT GO WITH TMOBILE!!!!!!

Came to TMobile store to buy Iphone 13, 512 gb, which was not available in store, so Staff helped to place order. Gave them delivery address by typing it carefully through text msg and gave to them, but unfortunately they still put it incorrectly. Tried to spend more than half day of Sunday (3 calls and 3 email) between T Mobile customer service (TMCS) and USP to correct address, but they kept referring me to contact each other until TMCS' s supervisor promised to take care of this.
However, checking tracking delivery 2 days later it was still incorrect address. Called again for almost two hours to update correct address again and it was really fixed this time by returning the item back to warehouse. Operator confirmed to call back the day after at 8PM to instruct for further step. Receiving no phone call for 3 days, so I went back to TMobile store asking them for help and report the issue, which caused me too much times and problems. Staff referred me call TMCS again. I asked them for a report/complaint for this matter, but again they redirected to TMCS. I was so disappointed and notified to review their store/service/response. To my shock, they challenged me to do this and they was proud to confirm/reveal to me that they was Store Manager (SM). How could they be proud of themself as SM when they was at least unable to do a basic and simple thing to apologize their loyal customer for what customer had been through?!? Not a word! Unprofessional and bad service!
After every call that I made, they checked for a review/survey. I put a good review based on their promises, but turned out problem was not fixed to its root. What was a true meaning of survey/review without a real outcome and solution??? It is worth as a trick. So frustrated!!!

I been with T mobile for 17 years I never had a problem, obviously that's  why I been with them so long. All the Tmobile experts are very professional and very polite But today on 4/29/22  around 9:30 am I received a Text message my bank account will be charge for $ 322.93 by Tmobile . I was  shocked with unusual fee and called Tmobile and requested  manager to my surprise the manager name Cadice was extremely Rude and seems like she is having horrible day . She was very aggressive and yelling at me to the point I couldn't believe my ears.  She told me I don't really have option but to deal with my problem and hang up the phone on me ! I sat   in my office frozen in disbelieve . I called back again minutes later requested manager to explain the misunderstanding but I came across with the manager name judy and expert name jc. There were Amazing . They figured it  was technically issue and were able to refund my money. They were polite and professional. I really think Tmobile should train cutmer service and how to keep your personal issues at home and be professional at all times to the manager Candice prior to giving her position as a manager !!!!Lessons really needed !

To avoid people become a victim I did,from T-mobile. I strongly recommend do not buy or start any contract with them at all!! a simple cancellation process, took my 3 weeks of effort(like i am the employee and not the customer?!?) and over-charged amounts without providing clear answers when you asked to them. In the end you may find yourself without a product and paying amounts of that product, because eventhough you cancel your order, Tmobile is not a proffessional and try to steal as much as money they can get from you. Zero customer priority! This company is a scam. DONT DO ANY PURCHASES OR ANY CONTRACTS!!!

We were forced to T-mobile, both my husband and myself have been long time customers of Sprint with no issues. Once we were forced to T-Mobile we have had nothing but problem after problem. Billing issues and being lied to. My husband was told that he needed a new phone since his old phone did not have a SIM card. He was told that the phone would be NO charge, FREE! However the representative did not properly code it correctly and a fee has been assessed each month. 
My husband called and spoke to a representative trying to resolve it, but to no avail… he was promised a call back from a supervisor, however hours have passed and still no call! Apparently T-Mobile does not understand the concept of telling the truth and doing what they say! After careful consideration we have decided to sever all ties with T-Mobile.

$400 down payment, brand new samsung phones dont come with chargers and we went into a t-mobile store and they couldn't transfer our service. Worst experience buying a phone ever.

What kind of moron sells a phone with no charger or USB connection, dumbest thing ive ever seen...

edit: Also, the customer service is terrible took 4 phone calls and two in-store visits to transfer our service, this company turns the simplest things into all-day projects. Had to fight through two sales pitches at the store before I could activate my phone, now i just wish i picked a different provider.
horrible service dont recommend

Absolutely the worst provider!! I have a partner in the states that used T-Mobile for a year and a half and he had to get a new SIM card minimum twice a month because it was viewed as “suspected activity” to call to a European country every day.. their plans sucks and one employee of T-Mobile set his phone plan wrong last time so he ended up with a 700 dollar phone bill and froze his card after ONLY 5 hours on the phone with a brand new SIM card. Would never in a million years recommend T-Mobile, not even to my worst enemy!

My phone stopped working. They said the battery compartment showed it got wet. I told them that I did not get it wet. They sold me a new $750 phone. I then decided to have my phone looked at by a phone expert. $45 later it was working fine. It just needed cleaned. I told my salesman and they wouldn't take my new phone back after a couple weeks. T- Mobile is a crooked incompetent company, overpriced and should be put out of business. They sent my account to collections. I wouldn't give them another dime of my money if they were the only cell company on earth !

I used their Gateway for internet.  It didn’t work for our needs.  I cancelled my service before the next billing date.  I had to return the Gateway.  Could not bring it to the store.  They still charged me for the next months service, that I had already cancelled.  When I tried to get my account information to trouble shoot, my account did not exist and the customer service rep was unable to find it. After speaking to a third person, I was told as soon as I got confirmation they received the Gateway I would get an email with my refund information.  I got the email, refund says $0. Refund should be $50. I do not recommend using their services.  While the people working at the site are friendly, they have to call in for everything they do for you as a customer.  Something you could waste your time in the comfort of your own home.

I would never recommend anyone to T.mobile, they charged me for services I never used. Over a period of three months an agent created a line on my account that I never asked for and when I switched my service to another courier the number stayed on my account for three months, T-Mobile sent me a bill which I never used this was in reference to a number on an account that I thought was closed. And they sent this bill to collection in a single month.

Received email offer from them dated 4/14, wasn't able to use the link, second time this has happened. Called and used tge chat features only to be given a run around and lied to... you can't reach a US based representative only a Filipino call center that can't do anything but read scripts. Unfortunately I was forced to become a T-Mobile customer because they bought out Sprint. This company lies and refuses to honor the offers they send to customers.

edits.text:
I upgraded my phone in November of 2020, since I was a sprint customer I was told I should get the T-Mobile Sim after all sprint was now part of T-Mobile. The hell started as soon as I left the store. They supposedly didn't activate the phone correctly. It is now May 16th 2021, over 100 calls to support later, A brand new replacement phone because they claimed among many things my original phone had a defective Sim reader.
The issue, if I am driving or moving and change towers going from a 5g to 4g and back to 5g. I can't make or receive calls, texts, or use the web... I have to restart my phone.  
I have been told every excuse except what will be done to fix the service I pay for.
Every time I am told it's some tower issue. However it isn't.  That doesn't explain the loss of functions. From what I have been able to determine it is a serious systems issue that affects sprint customers on the T-Mobile network. But T-Mobile doesn't seem to want to address this issue. I even got through to a sprint employee not a T-Mobile who stated they don't listen to the sprint techs. They don't use the same trouble shooting documentation system so T-Mobile keeps closing tickets out with out fixing the problem and every call requires a new ticket that never gets escalated to anyone who will do anything about it.
I was told 6 months and it will be fixed. It's past that and yet I am still being forced to pay for service that maybe works 50% of the time as well as spending hours trying to get through to a supervisor which the rude support staff won't do they just make excuses.
Don't waste your money on them.

text: Since being forced to upgrade phone to use new 5g network my service is TERRIBLE. Locations that I used to have coverage have very spotty service now; one bar at best! If the company doesn’t resolve this issue soon I am switching carriers.

Horrible customer service, it takes 43 minutes to speak to a supervisor, they are based in the Philippines, they bill me a bogus bill that I never had an account whit them, be very careful when you go to a store just to do a simple inquire about a plan, they take your personal info and it goes into their system, now you have an account with them, refuse to give your info just to ask a simple question about a plan, witch is very expensive and the advertising is very deceiving, and good luck when you try to call customer service, you never going to have an straight answer to your questions. Stay away from this company, Verizon is a little more expensive but their plan is straight forward, never an issued, and customer service is here in the US.

For the last few months, my wifes and my cell service is terrible.  Phones dont ring when calls come in, voice mail suddenly arrive.  Text messaging doesnt go through.  This has all taken place since the transition from Sprint to T Mobile.  We are long term customers, and elderly.  We rely on these phones.  T mobile customer service just want to sell you a new phone.  Our phones are 2 years old. Very disappointing customer service, and overall service. Not recommended.   👎👎

I have been trying to get this company to return the 2 extra deposits that they charged my credit card for a new Samsung S22 Ultra for over 8 weeks.  They admit that they did it as well as the insurance and set up charges.
I never ordered the insurance and I set up the phones.  I have spoken to at least 15 of their "Experts" and each one all the up to supervisors, in Birmingham and off shore, claims to take ownership of the problems, but never calls back.  This is not a reflection of the store personnel, who have tried to resolve the fraudulent charges, too no avail.
Calling the corporate customer service phone # gets you routed to the Philippines, which I am convinced is the black hole of corporate responsibility.
To add the cherry to this f'n ice cream sundae they charge $400 more than Samsung.

Update:  I had to get the President's office involved to fix the issues and to get my money back.  Their new gimmick a hustle to get insurance for your phone and two "experts" claimed Samsung won't honor their warranty without a hefty service NOT TRUE. Just another hustle for more revenue.  And they said AT&T was bad...

I purchased mobile hotspot with them.  Even though I canceled it they kept charging me.  I finally had to get the credit card company to investigate and they reversed the charges.  I had the email confirmation from when I canceled.  The service itself wasn't terrible, but there was times when the coverage wasn't nearly what they claim on their maps.  The 5G service is fast though so if you need that and they have coverage you won't be disappointed.

I normally don’t write reviews, but I thought I should to warn other people and hopefully help this from happening again. I have been an ATT cell phone customer for 10+ year, but finally decided to move over to T-Mobile to save money and get a new phone. I called a T-Mobile location and asked if I had to go to a store or if I can setup my service online and the representative told me I had to go into a store to set up a new account. So I went to the T-Mobile store. I spoke to a representative who helped me. While he was setting my account, he told me that I would get charged a $30 activation fee for setting up the new service, but that I could later call customer service to get the charge waved. The representative asked me to write my SS# on a piece of paper so that he could fill out the contact and start my service. I told him that I was not writing my SS number down and that I would rather have him give me the tablet when it was time to input the SS number. (NEVER GIVE YOUR SS# WHENEVER POSSIBLE!) Then I needed to pay for the activation service and phone. I handed my credit card and he charged it. All was done and I left the store. That very same day my credit was used and continued to be charged for 3 days and it was charged over $500 for local items near my area, like restaurants, convenience stores, etc. I immediately locked my card and reported these fraudulent charges to my credit card company. This was the only person that I gave my credit card to before and after. I  did not give my card to any other merchant server days before or after. So I know this is the person who stole it and made these purchases. I called T-Mobile customer service and reported the incidence. I also inquired about getting credited for the $30 activation charge as I was told by the sales representative at the store. The T-Mobile representative on the phone told me that they do not credit the charge or have any programs to do so, and I could have avoided it if I did it myself online. He also said that the sales representative also should have told me this. I told the representative on the phone that the sales representative did not disclose that information to me and in fact told me I had to come in the store to do all this. The representative on the phone was nice, but did not credit me the $30. So I felt even more scammed by this company. My card was stolen, got charged over 500 bucks, I have to get a new credit card, I have to change billing method for several bills now, etc. I had to go through all this hassle and still get stiffed for $30, which according to the represented I spoke to, is not an activation charge but a charge for using a person in the store, which I could have avoided. BEWARE OF T-MOBILE and any other merchants asking for your credit card. It’s hard to avoid it, but try or keep an eye on your account for unauthorized charges. I am questioning my decision to move over the T-Mobile because I feel cheated and lied to. This is very disappointing and bad business.

I had a T-Mobile phone rep promise a $700 credit on IPhone13 to add a line plus $15 additional line charge for service. Told her about my experience when I migrated from Sprint, with a Rep in the Mt Kisco NY store. He Lied to me about the trade in value for my sprint phone. The phone Rep was like “well that’s not going to happen when you call into over the phone sales, we are on a “recorded line”. Turns out……All lies, no discounts applied, I financed the 2nd phone in full. There were no monthly credits on the bill to reduce the cost of the new phone.BTW  $70/mo. for service on 2nd line not $15, Rep assuring me we were on a recorded line disarmed me. When I got a look at my bill no discounts! I Called 4/15 to find out the Best part!  T-Mobile doesn’t have access to the recordings!! They promised me a call today 4/18 which they did. The rep said they can’t do anything about it, then I asked transferred to his supervisor Abner (Employee ID: 2001840506) I am waiting for a call from Abner’s manager as of 1:30pm.
I was promised a call back within 2 hours. No call yet. I guess the store employees know they can say what they want, the phone reps can say what they want on recorded lines knowing full well the recordings are saved on 8-track tape and stored in the Ark of the Covenant. @mike.sievert

We did a free trial of T Mobile Internet.  Kept freezing so we declined and sent the equipment back.  Now we have received a bill for services not rendered.  Have called, chatted and dealt with local store.  No one can help and everyone tells us to call someone else.  Incredibly bad customer service and we have a bill that we do not owe!

T mobile owes me $150 dollars and I was told to contact customer service to receive It. The customer service reps kept asking me for a code that I was never given and I say reps because I spoke to 7 different people in which all told me they had cannot give me the money unless if I had a code or to go to a t mobile store. As I go into the store to verify my ID, they still refused to give me the money after arguing with each one for roughly 30 minutes. Then long after I removed my plan and went to Verizon, they kept trying to charge me for a plan I did not have! This company is nothing but a waste of time and are nothing but crooks. Go anywhere but here.

Went to T -Mobile. Several displays stating I phone on us. Talked to lady and got the 55.00 a month package. First statement, 103 dollars. I go back and I didn’t say I was trading in my old phone. Well!!!! They didn’t say to get the I phone 13 on them I had to say I was trading in my old phone. After 4 , 30 mile round trips I did get the 55.00 dollar a month deal but I have to pay 33 dollars a month for 24 months.
t-mobile.com sucks  They are crooks and Cheats and lie and lie and lie. And they’ve been this way for years. And I am so sick of them. Please do not waste your time or money with them.

I have been trying to deal with TMobile’s incredibly poor customer service since 4/1. Tech support is laughable, today I had a so-called “tech expert” tell me he didn’t know who ATT was. Since the first of the month the router they sent has been capping my data usage even though I am supposedly paying for an unlimited data plan. After 45 minutes, the service drops my service because they are capping “unlimited  data.” The router never worked correctly, the “replacement” they sent was dead out of the box. I was sent 88 EMAILS WITH RETURN LABELS for a replacement label. I have been repeatedly disconnected from calls with “tech support” after anywhere from 20-45 minutes with no resolution. These people obviously have no idea what they are doing, don’t care, and are fraudulently billing their service as “unlimited.” DO NOT BOTHER WITH THIS INCOMPETENT SERVICE.

At first they were ok. Then I replaced my phone last week along with insurance.  Phone was not working right from day one. I was not able to get back to the store for 16 days. They told me they could not replace it because it was over 14 days but the insurance would replace it. Long story short T-Mobile/Sprint bounced Mr back and forth for 3 days and over 6 hours. Now it's been sent up for expedited review. That is supposed to take 5 to 7 days. Real expediting right!! Been over a week now and I just keep getting call backs telling me no new update but will call me back in a few days. My bill is coming due and bet they expect it paid in full, but I still don't have a working phone. If you want good customer service I would go with someone else.

Can barely understand people on the phone,  they tell you there are options but it is only 1 option that only benefits t mobile.  Horrible customer care. I will not be coming back to  t mobile.  I can get better results using carrier pigeons.

After being very happy with T-Mobile's service for 7.5 years, I can not believe that I have spent 1.5 months in customer service hell. I went into the store on 2/13/22 and was signed up for a plan (without my knowledge) that I specifically requested not to be placed into. Then, I received a follow up call from customer service to get feedback on my experience in the store. I told them what happened and proceeded for another 2 months to attempt to get my planned reverted back to the original plan (which I was then told no longer existed). I spoke to a customer service rep who took all of my credit card information and then hung up on me. I immediately called back and found out that they should have never requested my credit card info...It's probably now on the dark web. A supervisor then told me to go into the store to change my account. The first store told me that I had to go to a corporate store. I drove to the corporate store immediately after and they said that I had to call. Then, after spending $4.75/gallon driving around to no avail, I have three customer service reps treat me like I am unable to read a bill and one offer me a $20 credit like it was a miracle for God. This is after they overcharged me $100. I've noticed that customer service has been suffering, but I never expected that after speaking to thirteen T-Mobile reps, that my loyalty is not at all valued (only three of them were nice and really attempted to help).

Stay away you can’t imagine or calculate how many hours in your life you will be spending on the phone with customer service the can’t give you a contract at the store they have nothing in writing saying that they will pay your promotions the still charge you for the suposingly 130$ discount on the I phone and they still charge you for the ear buds that we’re supposed to be free when you became a new customer . Be wear the firs bill is what they told you at the store then the second bill goes up just a little then the third bill comes in and bang all the free stuff isn’t free any more and the promotion has now expired but on there web site if you a new customer you can still get the exact same promotion that has expired and then your plan was written wrong the sales person didn’t process your plan correctly gave you the wrong one so your bill is really 65 dollars more and that protection plan that you got for every device at the store is not what you really have and we will give you credits but then your bill goes up again now you don’t qualify for that device oh and you don’t get Netflix any more and wait your bill is going up again because you were on the wrong plan I have over 30 hours of my life trying to get my bill back to what it was when I started and have been able to get the bill to that point but I have not been able to get my protection plan back and i still have  not gotten them to credit 260 dollars on my 2 apple watches I’m still paying for even though I paid for them at the store in full the day I left the store stay away unless you have nothing else important in your life and your rich

Not sure if it is because of the sprint merger but I have just canceled a phone upgrade and will be visiting Verizon tomorrow to change carriers. Went to the T-Mobil website as wife was having problems with her iphone 6 and wanted to do an upgrade. Decided on SE(3rd generation), agent advised that we needed to go online and order the upgrade as he had none in stock. Came home went online, attempted to log into my account, once again would not accept password, tried to reset password via security questions; no go: tried 3 times via code by text; never got the temporary password via text. Finally just went to webpage and went with phone upgrade. Eventually got to talk to an agent via chat. During the course of the next hour, unable to clarify some of my questions to her, I called the local agent and had him explain it to me, with the explanations ended up ordering a SE(3rdgen) for x $$ next day air charges and taxes. First mistake was her addition called her out on that and thought everything was good to go expecting to receive the phone in a couple of days. Later on in checking my account I see that the credit card and order was billed for second day  and not next day but the worse was that the phone wasn't going to be shipped untill the 25th, over 2 weeks away but was billed for 2nd day delivery. In calling them spent over an hour either on hold or explaining that they were advertising this phone on their website as available now and that is what I expected along with the next day air delivery I had authorized. Ended up they were saying that they couldn't access my account and could not cancel the order in order to order me one in another color. So much for their expert reps and we will do whatever we need to to make things right. I made them right for them, under federal law I have 3 days to cancel and you can charge me no penalties or other charges for me canceling. Canceled auto bill also. Verizon here we come.

We had Sprint and wanted new phones so now we’re under T-Mobile. Ever since we changed service my husband and I get numerous emails and texts from sources we don’t know!!!!! This is constant we’re sick of it!!!!
